steps.title: Find the per-unit decay factor
steps.content:
When $w$ increases by 1, the exponent $2w$ increases by 2. The ratio of consecutive outputs is:
$$\frac{z(w+1)}{z(w)} = \frac{(0.826)^{2w+2}}{(0.826)^{2w}} = (0.826)^2$$
Each unit increase in $w$ multiplies $z(w)$ by $(0.826)^2$.

steps.title: Compute the decay factor
steps.content: $(0.826)^2 = 0.682276 \approx 0.682$. So $z(w)$ retains about $68.2\%$ of its value after each unit increase in $w$.

steps.title: Convert to a percent decrease
steps.content: If $z(w)$ retains $68.2\%$, it decreases by $100\% - 68.2\% = 31.8\%$. So $p \approx 31.8$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) 0.174: Far too small — this would mean the function barely decreases. Eliminated.
  • B) 0.826: This is the base of the original function, not the percent decrease. Eliminated.
  • C) 17.4: This would be $1 - 0.826 = 0.174 = 17.4\%$, the decrease per unit increase in $2w$, not in $w$. Eliminated.
  • D) 31.8: Matches the computed $100(1 - 0.826^2) \approx 31.8\%$. Correct.
That matches Choice D — 31.8.
